HOME.page.title.message: OOMOL - Start with oo-cli. Build in Studio. Deliver through Cloud.

HOME.page.description.message: Install oo-cli through the official path so Codex, OpenClaw, Claude Code, and other agents can search, inspect, and run published tools first. When you need to build, combine, and deliver your own tools, move into OOMOL Studio and Cloud.

HOME.FirstScreen.heroTitle.message:
Your agent thinks and decides.
OOMOL handles the rest.

HOME.FirstScreen.heroLead.message: Start by connecting agents through oo-cli to third-party services like Gmail and Notion, plus your own systems. When packaged capabilities are not enough, use OOMOL Studio to build and extend your own tools.

HOME.PainPoints.title.message: Connect the tools you love without changing habits

HOME.PainPoints.subtitle.message: GitHub, Slack, Notion, Gmail, and your own APIs can work together through OOMOL, so agents can help without asking your team to change how they already work.

HOME.PainPoints.card1.title.message: Summarize a GitHub PR and send it to Slack

HOME.PainPoints.card1.description.message: Useful when you want to turn PR review into a team update without manual summarizing.

HOME.PainPoints.card1.prompt.message: Summarize the key points in this PR and send them to a Slack channel.

HOME.PainPoints.card1.solution.message: Read the diff, extract key points, and post to the target channel.

HOME.PainPoints.card2.title.message: Turn a Notion spec into Linear tasks

HOME.PainPoints.card2.description.message: Useful when you want to turn a spec page into trackable work without manual breakdown.

HOME.PainPoints.card2.prompt.message: Read this Notion page, break it into tasks, and sync them to Linear.

HOME.PainPoints.card2.solution.message: Extract requirements, shape tasks, and write them back to Linear.

HOME.PainPoints.card3.title.message: Read a Gmail attachment, then call your API

HOME.PainPoints.card3.description.message: Useful when you want to pipe email inputs straight into your own service.

HOME.PainPoints.card3.prompt.message: Read the Gmail attachment, call our PDF API, and return the result.

HOME.PainPoints.card3.solution.message: Download the attachment, call your API, and return the output.

HOME.FirstScreen.install.note.unix.message: Click to copy the oo-cli install command for your system.

HOME.FirstScreen.install.note.windows.message: Click to copy the oo-cli install command for Windows PowerShell.

HOME.BrandAssets.subtitle.message: OOMOL trademarks and logos must not be modified or used for anything other than representing OOMOL Inc.

HOME.GetStartedPrompt.title.message: Start with published tools, build your own only when needed

HOME.GetStartedPrompt.subtitle.message: Use oo-cli to get published tools working first. When you need custom ones, move into Studio and Cloud to compose, extend, and deliver your own tools.

HOME.Downloads.studioSection.title.message: Download OOMOL Studio when you want to build your own tools

HOME.Downloads.studioSection.subtitle.message: Use Studio when published tools are not enough. Build, combine, edit, and validate your own tools locally, then hand them to Cloud when you are ready to deliver them.

HOME.Downloads.cli.info.title.message: Start with oo-cli if you want to use tools from the terminal

HOME.Downloads.cli.info.description.message: If you want to search, run, and reuse tools in Codex, Claude Code, or your local terminal, start with oo-cli. Install it through the official install commands, or let your agent handle setup and verification.

HOME.Downloads.cli.method.agent.descript.message: Paste this prompt into your agent if you want it to use the official install commands and verify the setup for you.

HOME.Downloads.cta.title.message: Easy to install. Easy to extend.

HOME.Downloads.cta.description.message: Start with oo-cli when you only need to use tools. If you prefer a GUI, use OOMOL AI. Download OOMOL Studio when you want to build your own.

HOME.Community.subtitle.message: Get updates, share feedback directly, and follow the open-source projects we continue to maintain.

HOME.Community.slogan-1.message: Get product and release updates early

HOME.Community.slogan-2.message: Share problems and real-world use cases directly

HOME.Community.slogan-3.message: Track the open-source projects we maintain

HOME.Community.slogan-4.message: Every suggestion helps improve the product and docs

HOME.Community.open-source.subtitle.message: These projects reflect OOMOL's ongoing work on runtime, deployment, and real-world developer tools.

PRICING.subtitle.message: Build and validate locally for free. Free includes 200 Cloud Task minutes, Auth Link, and 20K requests through it each month. Upgrade only when you need more hosted runtime or a higher request volume.

PRICING.model.summary.message: Start in Studio for free, then decide whether you need cloud runtime or Auth Link. Free refreshes 200 Cloud Task minutes and 20K Auth Link requests every month.

PRICING.model.local.title.message: Build and validate in Studio for free

PRICING.model.local.text.message: Use the full local environment, run the tool with your own dependencies and workflow, and connect your own model if you already have one.

PRICING.model.online.title.message: Go live first, then scale runtime and request volume

PRICING.model.online.text.message: Free already lets you publish and use Cloud with 200 monthly Cloud Task minutes plus 20K Auth Link requests. Auth Link lets users connect accounts and grant access. Only requests made after authorization are billed.

PRICING.subscription.subtitle.message: Free covers local work and light hosted usage. Pro, Growth, and Scale add more Cloud Task runtime and more Auth Link request volume.

PRICING.subscription.free.description.message:
For local development,
validation, and getting your first tool online
with 200 Cloud Task minutes and 20K Auth Link requests

PRICING.subscription.free.feature1.message: Full OOMOL Studio local development environment

PRICING.subscription.free.feature2.message: Bring your own model service if you do not want managed AI billing

PRICING.subscription.free.feature3.message: Only calls to local function code in Studio avoid Cloud Task billing

PRICING.subscription.free.feature4.message: 200 Cloud Task minutes refreshed automatically every month

PRICING.subscription.free.feature5.message: Publish up to 20 packages total, public and private combined

PRICING.subscription.free.feature6.message: Auth Link included, plus 20K requests each month

PRICING.subscription.pro.description.message:
For solo builders in production
who need more hosted runtime
and a higher monthly request allowance

PRICING.subscription.pro.feature1.message: Publish online with OOMOL-managed delivery and runtime

PRICING.subscription.pro.feature2.message: 1,800 Cloud Task minutes included each month

PRICING.subscription.pro.feature3.message: 50K Auth Link requests included each month

PRICING.subscription.pro.feature4.message: A better fit for steady production traffic

PRICING.subscription.pro.feature5.message: $3 in general credits included each month

PRICING.subscription.pro.feature6.message: Publish up to 100 packages total, public and private combined

PRICING.subscription.growth.description.message:
For steady hosted traffic
that needs more monthly capacity
and a larger request allowance

PRICING.subscription.growth.feature1.message: All Pro capabilities, with more included usage

PRICING.subscription.growth.feature2.message: 5,000 Cloud Task minutes included each month

PRICING.subscription.growth.feature3.message: 200K Auth Link requests included each month

PRICING.subscription.growth.feature4.message: A better fit for higher-frequency traffic

PRICING.subscription.growth.feature5.message: $8 in general credits included each month

PRICING.subscription.growth.feature6.message: Publish up to 300 packages total, public and private combined

PRICING.subscription.max.description.message:
For higher-volume hosted traffic
that needs more runtime headroom
and a lower request overage rate

PRICING.subscription.max.feature1.message: All Growth capabilities, with higher limits

PRICING.subscription.max.feature2.message: 15,000 Cloud Task minutes included each month

PRICING.subscription.max.feature3.message: 1M Auth Link requests included each month

PRICING.subscription.max.feature5.message: $15 in general credits included each month

PRICING.subscription.max.feature6.message: Publish up to 1,000 packages total, public and private combined

PRICING.tables.subtitle.message: This section covers four categories: LLMs, Cloud Task, Auth Link, and OOMOL's first-party APIs.

PRICING.tables.llm.note.message: Check this only when you call OOMOL-billed models directly. Rates update with the model configuration in the console. Units are credits per million tokens.

PRICING.tables.llm.error.message: LLM pricing is temporarily unavailable. Please refresh later or check console directly.

PRICING.tables.llm.empty.message: No LLM pricing data is available right now.

PRICING.tables.cloudTask.noteText.message: Billed by runtime. Anything under one minute is billed as one minute

PRICING.tables.cloudTask.alert.message: Use this for cloud functions or APIs you publish through OOMOL. Cloud Task is the runtime layer and is billed by runtime.

PRICING.tables.gateway.alert.message: Use this for services that need user authorization. Auth Link handles account connection and authorization, and only requests made after a user grants access are billed.

PRICING.tables.gateway.empty.message: No Auth Link pricing data is available right now.

PRICING.tables.gateway.freeNote.message: Includes Auth Link, OAuth callbacks, token refresh, and connection checks

PRICING.tables.gateway.proNote.message: Includes Auth Link and a higher monthly request allowance

PRICING.tables.gateway.growthNote.message: Includes Auth Link and a larger monthly request allowance

PRICING.tables.gateway.scaleNote.message: Includes Auth Link and the highest monthly request allowance

PRICING.tables.fusionApi.alert.message: These are first-party APIs provided directly by OOMOL, such as image generation, TTS, and STT. Charges are deducted in credits based on each service unit.

PRICING.tables.fusionApi.description.nan.message: Nano Banana image generation or editing
Nano Banana Pro image generation or editing

CLOUD.hero.title.message: OOMOL Cloud - Publish tools for AI agents

CLOUD.hero.description.message: Build and test tools in Studio, then publish them to Cloud so they run online and can be found and used by AI agents through oo-cli.

STUDIO.page.title.message: OOMOL Studio - Generate, compose, and extend tools for oo-cli

STUDIO.page.description.message: When ready-made tools are not enough, use Studio's built-in AI to generate code and flows, then compose, extend, and ship them back through oo-cli and Cloud.

STUDIO.manifesto.title.message:
OOMOL Studio helps you
generate, compose, and extend tools

STUDIO.manifesto.subtitle.message: Use Studio's built-in AI to create or extend tools with workflows, then deliver them to Cloud for oo-cli use.

STUDIO.product.story.title.message:
When ready-made tools are not enough,
custom extension often gets blocked on these three points

STUDIO.product.story.description.message: Sustainable expansion depends on three things: manageable learning cost, predictable deployment, and ecosystem alignment.

STUDIO.product.principles.title.message:
How OOMOL Studio
solves these three problems

STUDIO.product.principles.description.message: The goal is not to add another platform abstraction, but to make the development flow smoother and easier to maintain.

STUDIO.product.cta.title.message: Get the next tool running in Studio first

STUDIO.product.cta.description.message: Build, debug, and get the tool working in a real coding environment first. When you are ready to deliver it, hand it to Cloud.

STUDIO.story.point1.description.message: Studio includes a built-in Agent, so you can start from intent instead of learning every concept first.

STUDIO.story.point2.description.message: A built-in container keeps local and cloud environments aligned, so dev, debug, and release stay on one track.

STUDIO.story.point3.description.message: When dependency management and design patterns diverge from community conventions, team knowledge is harder to reuse and extension costs rise.

Theme.Navbar.product.studio.description.message: Build, combine, and orchestrate your own tools when connected services and published tools are not enough

Theme.Navbar.product.cli.description.message: Primary entry: install officially, then search, inspect, and use tools directly

Theme.Navbar.product.cloud.description.message: Deliver tools back through oo-cli with hosted cloud runtime

Theme.Navbar.product.ai.description.message: Optional GUI: use the same capabilities through a chat interface

STUDIO.principle1.title.message: Built-in Studio Agent for generation and orchestration

STUDIO.principle1.paragraph1.message: 1. From node generation to workflow orchestration, Studio's built-in Agent handles the first pass so you can start immediately.

STUDIO.principle1.paragraph2.message: 2. It runs with full Studio context, not generic chat context, so it understands how to generate, orchestrate, and extend tools.

STUDIO.principle1.paragraph3.message: 3. Bring your own model and token, and take manual control in code whenever needed.

STUDIO.principle1.imageAlt.message: Write function tools with code in OOMOL Studio

STUDIO.principle2.title.message: Built-in high-performance container, unified local to cloud

STUDIO.principle2.paragraph1.message: 1. Studio ships with a high-performance container and common runtimes ready out of the box.

STUDIO.principle2.paragraph2.message: 2. Local and cloud share the same Linux base, so development, debugging, and release run on one consistent environment.

STUDIO.principle2.paragraph3.message: 3. The container is isolated from your host, so AI can write and run code without touching your local environment or private data.

STUDIO.principle2.imageAlt.message: Developer experience for function tools in OOMOL Studio

STUDIO.principle3.paragraph1.message: 1. In Studio, a node is a function. Nodes orchestrate into workflows, then package into reusable tools.

STUDIO.principle3.paragraph2.message: 2. Use npm for JavaScript and Poetry or pip for Python; dependency management follows community-standard practice.

STUDIO.principle3.paragraph3.message: 3. So even if AI does most of the work, human takeover stays straightforward in the same familiar workflow.

STUDIO.principle3.imageAlt.message: Local and cloud continuity for tools built in OOMOL Studio

HOME.ToolStrip.description.message: OOMOL comes with support for {providerCount} apps and {actionCount} packaged tools. They are not scattered APIs, but work entries agents can call directly through oo-cli. After common work is running, decide what is worth orchestrating further, extending, or turning into your own tool.

HOME.ToolStrip.providerStat.text.message: Covering common services across collaboration, development, marketing, and payments.

HOME.ToolStrip.actionStat.text.message: Not raw APIs, but ready-to-use actions.

HOME.CliReasons.title.message: One connection path for every agent

HOME.CliReasons.subtitle.message: Connect accounts, apps, and tool capabilities to OOMOL first, then let Codex, Claude Code, OpenClaw, and terminal workflows call them through the same oo-cli path.

HOME.CliReasons.card1.title.message: Let your agent install the CLI

HOME.CliReasons.card1.description.message: Copy the prompt on the right into Codex, Claude Code, or OpenClaw. It will run the official install command for your environment, then handle sign-in and checks.

HOME.CliReasons.card2.description.message: When ready-made tools are not enough, use OOMOL Studio to combine third-party tools, cloud functions, and APIs, or build a new tool. Published tools stay available through oo-cli.

HOME.CliReasons.installTerminal.agentPro.message: # copy the prompt to your agent so it can install and verify

HOME.CliReasons.installTerminal.installe.message: # oo-cli is installed in the current environment

HOME.CliReasons.installTerminal.loginRea.message: # sign in with your OOMOL account and confirm the CLI is ready

HOME.CliReasons.installTerminal.nextComm.message: # next: search for available tools and start running tasks

HOME.LinearFlow.studio.title.message: When packaged tools are not enough, generate, compose, and validate in OOMOL Studio

HOME.LinearFlow.studio.description.message: When ready-made tools are not enough, use Studio's built-in AI to generate new tools or extend existing ones with workflows, then deliver them to Cloud for oo-cli use.

HOME.LinearFlow.cloud.eyebrow.message: Developer path 02 / Run it in Cloud

HOME.LinearFlow.cloud.title.message: After validation, run your tool in Cloud

HOME.LinearFlow.cloud.description.message: Cloud runs the tool, stores config and secrets, manages access, and tracks usage. You do not need to build a separate backend service for the same tool.

HOME.LinearFlow.cloud.card1.title.message: Publish the tool and keep it running

HOME.LinearFlow.cloud.card1.text.message: Use the same tool for yourself, your team, or customers without copying and maintaining separate versions.

HOME.LinearFlow.cloud.card2.title.message: Manage config, access, and usage in one place

HOME.LinearFlow.cloud.card2.text.message: Secrets, permissions, versions, runtime config, and usage logs live in Cloud, making updates and troubleshooting easier.

HOME.LinearFlow.cloud.quota.text.message: Free users get 200 Cloud Task minutes each month. You can launch first without buying servers or reserving capacity.

HOME.LinearFlow.cli.title.message: After delivery, make it ready for agents

HOME.LinearFlow.cli.description.message: Once the tool is built and delivered, agents in Codex, OpenClaw, and Claude Code keep using the same oo-cli path to search for it, inspect it, and call it. For users, published tools and custom tools end up at the same entry point.

HOME.LinearFlow.cta.title.message: Start with published tools, then enter the developer path when they are not enough

HOME.LinearFlow.cta.description.message: Use oo-cli to get connected services and published tools working first. Only when you need to produce, combine, and deliver your own tools do you move into Studio and Cloud.

HOME.GuiEntry.title.message: You can keep using the same tools through OOMOL AI

HOME.GuiEntry.description.message: Once you have tools connected through oo-cli, OOMOL AI gives you the official GUI for using those same capabilities across web, desktop, and iOS.

HOME.CloudBenefits.title.message: Publish your first tool with the included quota

HOME.CloudBenefits.subtitle.message: Free users get 200 Cloud Task minutes each month. Use them to publish and try your first tool, then top up or upgrade when usage grows.

HOME.CloudBenefits.card1.title.message: Publish a first version people can use

HOME.CloudBenefits.card1.description.message: Use the monthly quota to put the tool online, check that it runs reliably, and make sure agents can find and run it through oo-cli.

HOME.CloudBenefits.card1.note.message: A good fit for first tools, lightweight jobs, and everyday trials.

HOME.CloudBenefits.card2.title.message: Pay only for the time you use

HOME.CloudBenefits.card2.description.message: You do not need to buy servers or reserve capacity in advance. After the free quota runs out, billing is based on actual run time, so you can grow when demand grows.

HOME.CloudBenefits.card2.note.message: Start with the included quota, then scale only when you need to.

APP.page.title.message: OOMOL AI - Use OOMOL tools in a visual workspace

APP.page.description.message: Use connected OOMOL tools in a conversational GUI across web, desktop, and iOS. Review results, ask follow-up questions, and keep tasks moving across devices.

APP.hero.lead.message: Use OOMOL AI on web, desktop, and iOS to start tasks through chat, review results, and decide what to do next. If your accounts, tools, and services are already connected, those capabilities are ready to use in the GUI.

APP.hero.productScreenshotAlt.message: OOMOL AI interface where a task is completed through chat and tool use

APP.models.title.message: Start the task, review the result, and keep going in one place

APP.models.description.message: Connected accounts, tool calls, task results, and follow-up questions stay in the same visual workspace. You can review the output first, then decide how to refine, ask, or continue.

APP.models.demo.title.message: No need to start over in another entry point

APP.models.demo.description.message: Open OOMOL AI and continue reviewing, asking, and working around the same task.

APP.models.demo.imageAlt.message: OOMOL AI workspace showing tool capabilities and mini apps

APP.models.cards.chat.title.message: Describe the goal, then move the task forward

APP.models.cards.chat.body.message: Say what you want to get done in natural language. Results and next actions stay on the page, so it is easy to keep going.

APP.models.cards.image.title.message: See the output clearly before choosing the next step

APP.models.cards.image.body.message: Images, documents, spreadsheets, and intermediate results are easier to review on the page before you refine the request or continue.

APP.problems.cards.review.title.message: When you need to review the result before continuing

APP.problems.cards.review.body.message: Images, documents, spreadsheets, long text, and intermediate outputs are easier to inspect in a GUI. Once the result looks right, you can add more context and continue.

APP.problems.cards.iterate.title.message: When the task takes more than one pass

APP.problems.cards.iterate.body.message: Many tasks need several rounds: generate a first version, revise it, ask follow-up questions, and add constraints. OOMOL AI is built for that kind of continued work.

APP.problems.cards.devices.title.message: When you want to continue across devices

APP.problems.cards.devices.body.message: Handle complex work on desktop, open it quickly in a browser, or check progress from your iPhone. The entry point changes, but the OOMOL capabilities stay the same.

APP.workflow.steps.describe.title.message: Say what you want to get done

APP.workflow.steps.describe.body.message: Describe the goal in natural language, and OOMOL AI helps move the task forward in the current conversation.

APP.workflow.steps.describe.detail.message: Useful when you do not want to move every step back into the terminal.

APP.workflow.steps.describe.alt.message: OOMOL AI interface where a task starts from chat

APP.workflow.steps.review.title.message: Review the result, then choose the next step

APP.workflow.steps.review.body.message: Results stay on the page, so you can ask follow-up questions, change the request, or continue the task.

APP.workflow.steps.review.detail.message: A better fit for work that moves forward through review and iteration.

APP.workflow.steps.review.alt.message: OOMOL AI interface continuing a multi-step tool task

APP.workflow.steps.devices.title.message: Switch devices and keep the task in view

APP.workflow.steps.devices.body.message: Use it on web, desktop, or iOS when the same task needs attention in different settings.

APP.workflow.steps.devices.detail.message: Work through complex tasks at your desk, then check progress later from another device.

APP.workflow.steps.devices.alt.message: OOMOL AI interface continuing work with the same OOMOL capabilities

APP.outputs.title.message: Run fast in the terminal. Review clearly in the GUI.

APP.outputs.description.message: oo-cli is a strong fit for searching, inspecting, and running tools from the terminal and agent workflows. OOMOL AI is for the moments when you need result previews, follow-up conversation, and continuity across devices while using the same capabilities.

APP.outputs.imageAlt.message: OOMOL AI interface continuing work with the same tools in one workspace

APP.pricing.description.message: OOMOL AI and oo-cli share the same account and credits. Whether a task starts from the terminal, web, desktop, or iOS, usage is counted the same way, so you do not need to top up each entry point separately.

APP.pricing.packs.starter.description.message: Includes 5 credits. A small refill for light use or occasional top-ups.

APP.pricing.packs.starter.feature1.message: Useful for a small first balance

APP.pricing.packs.starter.feature2.message: No need to reserve too much upfront

APP.pricing.packs.boost.description.message: Includes 24 credits (20 base + 4 bonus). A better fit for regular day-to-day use.

APP.pricing.packs.boost.feature1.message: A stronger fit for steady daily use

APP.pricing.packs.boost.feature2.message: More comfortable when several tasks arrive in a row

APP.pricing.packs.ultra.description.message: Includes 128 credits (100 base + 28 bonus). Best for batch work and heavier periods.

APP.pricing.packs.ultra.feature1.message: Built for batch work and longer workflows

APP.pricing.packs.ultra.feature2.message: Keeps more headroom available during heavier periods

APP.downloads.title.message: Open it on the device you need

APP.downloads.items.web.description.message: Start in the browser without installing anything, then review and continue your task.

APP.downloads.items.desktop.description.message: Best when you want to keep a workspace open for longer or more complex tasks.

APP.downloads.items.ios.description.message: Track progress and continue the current task from your phone or tablet.

CLI.page.description.message: Install and update oo, search and inspect tools, run connector actions and cloud tasks, and manage skills and files from one CLI so agents can get to work.

CLI.hero.overview.message: Agents can analyze and plan, but execution still depends on real-world tools. oo-cli connects them to those tools so they can actually get work done.

CLI.hero.installNote.unix.message: Click to copy the oo-cli install command for your system.

CLI.hero.installNote.windows.message: Click to copy the oo-cli install command for Windows PowerShell.

CLI.hero.terminal.loginReady.message: # sign in with your OOMOL account and confirm the CLI is ready

CLI.hero.terminal.searchQr.message: $ oo search "generate a QR code for OOMOL"

CLI.hero.terminal.searchTogether.message: # searches packages and connector actions together

CLI.hero.terminal.narrowPackages.message: # narrow it down to published packages before choosing one

CLI.hero.terminal.inspectPackage.message: # inspect schema, version, and runnable blocks before execution

CLI.hero.terminal.fetchResult.message: # fetch the result back in the terminal

CLI.painpoints.title.message: Set it up, find a ready-made tool, then run the task

CLI.painpoints.subtitle.message: This page does not need to unpack every command. The main job is simpler: get oo-cli into the terminal, use it to find a ready-made tool, then run the task and bring the result back. The detailed command surface belongs in the docs.

CLI.painpoints.card1.title.message: Connect oo-cli to the terminal and host apps

CLI.painpoints.card1.description.message: Use the official install command to put oo in the terminal, then install the needed skills into Codex, Claude Code, and other supported hosts.

CLI.painpoints.card1.point2.message: Keep working from the same place

CLI.painpoints.card2.title.message: Search ready-made tools before building your own

CLI.painpoints.card2.description.message: Search packages and connectors first, then inspect package metadata or connector schemas to confirm whether a reusable capability already exists.

CLI.painpoints.card2.point1.message: Understand inputs and capability boundaries first

CLI.painpoints.card3.title.message: Run, wait for results, and inspect logs in one path

CLI.painpoints.card3.description.message: Short tasks can run directly through connectors. Longer jobs can go through Cloud Task. Waiting, fetching results, files, and logs all stay inside oo-cli.

CLI.painpoints.card3.point1.message: Supports dry-run and long task tracking

CLI.painpoints.card3.point2.message: No separate admin console needed to finish the job

CLI.flow.workflow.title.message: This is roughly how a real task gets moving

CLI.flow.workflow.description.message: Install and log in first, then search and confirm the tool. If it can run directly, run it directly. If it takes longer, wait for the result to come back. The detailed commands and parameters live in the docs.

CLI.flow.workflow.terminal.label.message: One real task usually looks like this

CLI.flow.workflow.terminal.comment1.message: # install oo-cli with the official install command, then sign in

CLI.flow.workflow.terminal.comment2.message: # search and confirm whether it is the right tool

CLI.flow.workflow.terminal.comment3.message: # run it directly, or wait for the longer job to come back

CLI.flow.next.title.message: When ready-made tools are not enough, use Studio or Cloud

CLI.flow.next.description.message: Let oo-cli establish the published-tool path and the install/update lifecycle first. If you need to build your own tool or keep it running continuously, then move on to Studio or Cloud.

CLI.flow.next.card1.title.message: Go to Studio to generate or complete your own tool

CLI.flow.next.card1.text.message: When existing tools are not enough, use Studio to let the agent generate code, fill in parameters, and validate the tool locally.

CLI.flow.next.card2.title.message: Go to Cloud when it needs stable runtime and delivery

CLI.flow.next.card2.text.message: When the tool needs stable runtime, logs, or broader reuse, let Cloud take over.

CLI.flow.cta.title.message: Let your agent use these tools now

CLI.flow.cta.description.message: Install oo-cli with the official install commands, then use it to search tools, inspect inputs, and run a real task from the terminal.

CLOUD.first.slogan.message:
Put delivery, hosted runtime,
and access control in Cloud

CLOUD.first.overview.message: Studio is where the tool gets built and validated. Cloud is where delivery happens, with hosted runtime, configuration, and access handled in one place, then used mainly through oo-cli by agents.

CLOUD.painpoints.title.message: Ship newly built tools to Cloud and keep them stable

CLOUD.painpoints.subtitle.message: In Studio, generate, combine, and extend tools around your business needs. After publishing to Cloud, runtime and access control are managed in one place.

CLOUD.painpoints.card1.title.message: Package generated and combined tools into new capabilities

CLOUD.painpoints.card1.description.message: In Studio, you can write code to create new function tools and combine existing ones. After publishing to Cloud, those capabilities become new tools that keep running reliably.

CLOUD.painpoints.card1.point1.message: Both generated and combined tools can be published directly

CLOUD.painpoints.card1.point2.message: Use richer custom tools through oo-cli

CLOUD.painpoints.card2.title.message: Access and permissions are managed by Cloud

CLOUD.painpoints.card2.description.message: No matter which agent you use, once it is connected through oo-cli it follows the same account and permission system. Your tool capabilities move with you instead of being tied to one agent.

CLOUD.painpoints.card2.point1.message: Accounts and permissions stay with your identity

CLOUD.painpoints.card2.point2.message: Keep the same toolset when switching agents

CLOUD.flow.cloud.title.message: Publish to Cloud and keep new tools stable

CLOUD.flow.cloud.description.message: Publish the tools you generated and combined in Studio to Cloud. After launch, Cloud handles runtime and access management so you do not have to maintain them separately.

CLOUD.flow.cli.title.message: After publishing, keep using them through oo-cli across agents

CLOUD.flow.cli.description.message: Users do not need to care about deployment details or change habits. With oo-cli connected, they can keep using the same toolset across different agents.

CLOUD.flow.cta.title.message: Publish new tools to Cloud, then keep using them through oo-cli

CLOUD.flow.cta.description.message: Cloud handles post-launch management, while your team keeps the same oo-cli workflow.

HOME.Downloads.page.description.message: Use oo-cli through its official install commands as the primary entry point for connecting agents to third-party services and published tools. If you prefer a GUI, use OOMOL AI. Download OOMOL Studio when you want to build your own tools.

HOME.Downloads.hero.title.message: Choose the OOMOL entry point that fits you best

HOME.Downloads.hero.subtitle.message: Start with oo-cli if you want agents to use tools in the terminal, and install it through the official install commands. Use OOMOL AI if you prefer a GUI. Download OOMOL Studio when you want to build your own tools.

HOME.Downloads.ai.title.message: If you prefer a GUI, use OOMOL AI

HOME.Downloads.ai.subtitle.message: OOMOL AI is the official GUI for the same tools, giving you an easier way to chat, review results, and keep working across web, desktop, and iOS. If you mainly want an easier way to use tools, start here.

PRICING.page.description.message: Build and validate locally for free. Free includes 200 Cloud Task minutes, Auth Link, and 20K requests through it each month. Upgrade only when you need more hosted runtime or a higher request volume.

SUPPORT.page.description.message: Get help through GitHub Discussions, Discord, or email.

SUPPORT.github.description.message: Go to our GitHub discussions to browse for help and best practices.

SUPPORT.discord.description.message: If you need instant communication, please join our Discord server.

SUPPORT.email.description.message: If you need help beyond the product, you can contact us by email.

TERMS.page.description.message: Read OOMOL's terms of service and usage conditions.

PRIVACY.page.description.message: Read OOMOL's privacy policy and data-handling terms.

HOME.BrandAssets.page.description.message: Download OOMOL logos, icons, color references, and type assets.

HOME.Community.page.description.message: Find OOMOL community links, open-source repositories, and contact channels.

HOME.Community.repo.pdfCraft.description.message: Convert PDFs into EPUB, Markdown, and other formats, with a strong focus on scanned books.

HOME.Community.repo.ovmWin.description.message: Run the ovm-core virtual machine on Windows through WSL2.

HOME.Community.repo.macPowerMonitor.desc.message: Receive macOS sleep and wake notifications.

COOKIE.message.message: We use optional cookies for site analytics and conversion tracking. Essential cookies remain enabled so the website can function correctly.

COOKIE.manage.message.message: Choose which optional cookies OOMOL may use on this device. You can reopen these settings from the footer at any time.

COOKIE.manage.essential.subtitle.message: Required for core site behavior such as language preference, security, and session continuity.

COOKIE.manage.analytics.subtitle.message: Helps us understand visits, traffic sources, and product usage across the website.

COOKIE.manage.advertising.subtitle.message: Allows Google Ads conversion tracking for key actions such as downloads and first sign-in.

HOME.Downloads.cli.installDescription.wi.message: Showing the Windows PowerShell install command for your current browser environment. See the docs for Windows CMD.

HOME.Downloads.cli.installDescription.un.message: Showing the curl install command for macOS / Linux based on your current browser environment.

HOME.Downloads.cli.agentPrompt.windows.message:
Please help me install and verify oo-cli:

1. Install oo-cli using the official method for this environment.
   - Windows PowerShell: `irm https://cli.oomol.com/install.ps1 | iex`

2. Run `oo login`.

3. Confirm that the CLI is ready to use.

4. Tell me the best next oo-cli command to run.

HOME.Downloads.cli.agentPrompt.unix.message:
Please help me install and verify oo-cli:

1. Install oo-cli using the official method for this environment.
   - macOS / Linux: `curl -fsSL https://cli.oomol.com/install.sh | bash`

2. Run `oo login`.

3. Confirm that the CLI is ready to use.

4. Tell me the best next oo-cli command to run.
